paper_title: A New V361A Mutation in Amaranthus palmeri PPX2 Associated with PPO-Inhibiting Herbicide Resistance
authors: Nie, HZ; Harre, NT; Young, BG
resistance_context: field survivors; candidate target-site mutation; bacterial functional assay
evidence_methods: genotyping; candidate mutation screening; transformed bacterial strain assay; resistance ratio reported for fomesafen
key_result_summary: V361A in PPX2 was identified in A. palmeri and experimentally tested for PPO-inhibitor resistance against lactofen and fomesafen.
page_note: PDF page 1 abstract; PPX2 and bacterial assay terms also visible in later methods/results pages.
notes: Paper-level evidence only; review full tables/figures before creating mutation-level rows.

paper_title: A Statewide Survey of PPO-Inhibitor Resistance and the Prevalent Target-Site Mechanisms in Palmer amaranth (Amaranthus palmeri) Accessions from Arkansas
authors: Varanasi, VK; Brabham, C; Norsworthy, JK; Nie, H; Young, BG; Houston, M; Barber, T; Scott, RC
resistance_context: statewide survey of Palmer amaranth accessions; target-site mechanisms after fomesafen screening
evidence_methods: fomesafen screen; TaqMan qPCR genotyping; field survey; greenhouse context
key_result_summary: Arkansas Palmer amaranth accessions were screened with fomesafen and genotyped for Delta G210 and R128G/M target-site mechanisms in PPX2.
page_note: PDF page 1 abstract; PDF page 3 Results and Discussion references Figure 1 and Table 1.
notes: Paper-level evidence only; mutation-level row creation deferred for manual review.

paper_title: Common target-site resistance mutations for PPO-inhibiting herbicides in waterhemp (Amaranthus tuberculatus) and Palmer amaranth (Amaranthus palmeri) do not confer cross-resistance to trifludimoxazin
authors: Steppig, NR; Johnson, WG; Young, BG
mutations_reported: Delta G210; R128G; R128M; R128I; V361A; G399A
resistance_context: greenhouse dose-response and field efficacy for known PPO-resistant biotypes and populations
evidence_methods: greenhouse dose-response; field experiments; resistant/susceptible ratio comparisons
key_result_summary: Known PPO2 target-site mutations in waterhemp and Palmer amaranth did not show cross-resistance to trifludimoxazin in the reported assays.
page_note: PDF page 1 abstract; PDF page 3 references Figure 1 and Table 1.
notes: Paper-level evidence only; some mutation terms are background/context and should be checked before mutation-level rows.

paper_title: Distribution of PPX2 Mutations Conferring PPO-Inhibitor Resistance in Palmer Amaranth Populations of Tennessee
authors: Copeland, JD; Giacomini, DA; Tranel, PJ; Montgomery, GB; Steckel, LE
resistance_context: Tennessee Palmer amaranth survey for known PPX2 mutations after fomesafen selection
evidence_methods: fomesafen selection; TaqMan qPCR; dCAPS genotyping; survey
key_result_summary: Tennessee Palmer amaranth populations contained Delta G210, R128G, and R128M PPX2 mutations associated with PPO-inhibitor resistance.
page_note: PDF page 1 abstract; PDF page 3 Results and Discussion references Figure 1 and Table 1.
notes: Paper-level evidence only; exact genotype frequencies should be reviewed before mutation-level rows.

paper_title: Distribution of target site PPO-inhibiting herbicide resistance mutations in waterhemp and Palmer amaranth and association of epyrifenacil efficacy with PPX2 target site variants
authors: Montgomery, J; Singh, D; Tyre, A; Chittoor, J; Wollam, C; Chiapelli, B; Pawlak, J; Aradhya, C; Perez-Jones, A
resistance_context: survey of Palmer amaranth and waterhemp accessions; association of PPX2 variants with epyrifenacil response

key_result_summary: Epyrifenacil controlled most sampled accessions, while PPX2 target-site variants were sequenced and associated with response metrics.

paper_title: Investigating target-site resistance mechanism to the PPO-inhibiting herbicide fomesafen in waterhemp and interspecific hybridization of Amaranthus species using next generation sequencing
authors: Nie, HZ; Mansfield, BC; Harre, NT; Young, JM; Steppig, NR; Young, BG
species_studied: Amaranthus tuberculatus; Amaranthus palmeri; Amaranthus albus
mutations_reported: Delta G210; R128G; R128I; R128K; R128M
resistance_context: PPO-resistant waterhemp populations and interspecific Amaranthus gene-flow evidence
evidence_methods: MiSeq amplicon sequencing; Sanger sequencing; bacterial expression assay; sequence alignment
key_result_summary: Next-generation sequencing identified R128G/I PPX2 mutations novel to waterhemp and provided evidence of Amaranthus gene flow.
page_note: PDF page 1 abstract and conclusion; methods/results sections discuss MiSeq, Sanger sequencing, and bacterial assays.
notes: Paper-level evidence only; R128K did not show the same bacterial-system confirmation in the abstract and needs careful manual handling.

paper_title: Target-site and metabolic mechanisms confer broad resistance to PPO-inhibiting herbicides in Amaranthus retroflexus
authors: Sun, ZH; Cao, WL; Wang, S; Du, L; Liu, C; Li, DL; Li, CJ; Feng, Y; Liu, XL; Zhou, ZT; Xu, XF; Sun, WP; Teng, CH
resistance_context: resistant A. retroflexus genotypes with target-site and non-target-site resistance mechanisms
evidence_methods: whole-plant bioassay; PPO enzyme activity assay; PPX2 sequencing; metabolism assay; transcriptome/RT-qPCR; heterologous expression
key_result_summary: An A. retroflexus genotype carried an Arg-128-Gly PPX2 mutation and the paper also reported metabolic resistance evidence for fomesafen.
page_note: PDF page 1 abstract/results; PDF page 10 conclusions.
notes: Paper-level evidence only; useful for Amaranthus spp. background but not a waterhemp or Palmer amaranth paper.

paper_title: Two new PPX2 mutations associated with resistance to PPO-inhibiting herbicides in Amaranthus palmeri
authors: Giacomini, DA; Umphres, AM; Nie, HZ; Mueller, TC; Steckel, LE; Young, BG; Scott, RC; Tranel, PJ
resistance_context: Palmer amaranth populations where Delta G210 did not fully explain PPO-inhibitor resistance
evidence_methods: PPX2 cDNA sequencing; fomesafen selection; dCAPS assay development
key_result_summary: Sequencing identified R98G and R98M PPX2 mutations in resistant A. palmeri plants, and a dCAPS assay was developed for detection.
notes: Paper-level evidence only; residue-number mapping and nomenclature should be checked before mutation-level rows.
